The non-believers insulted the Jews, as if their idols Bel and Nebo were too powerful for Jehovah. But their worshippers cannot help them; both the idols and the idolaters are now captives. Let God’s people not be afraid of either. Those things from which ungodly men expect safety and happiness will be found unable to save them from death and hell. The true God will never fail his worshippers. The history of the life of every believer is a kind of summary of the history of Israel. Our spiritual life is upheld by his grace, as constantly as our natural life by his providence. And God will never leave them. The Author will be the Finisher of their well-being, when, through decline, they need help as much as in infancy. This promise to Israel, weakened and old as a nation, applies to every aged follower of Christ. When surrounded with infirmities, and perhaps those around begin to grow weary of you, yet I am He that I have promised to be, He that you would have me to be. I will support you; carry you on in your way, and carry you home at last. If we learn to trust in and love him, we need not be anxious about our remaining days or years; he will still provide for us and watch over us, both as the creatures of his power, and as new-created by his Spirit.

Here the foolishness of those who made idols and then prayed to them is exposed. How does the generosity of idolaters shame the stinginess of many who call themselves God’s servants, but are for a religion which costs them nothing! The service of sin always costs a great deal. God shows them what senseless, helpless things idols are. Let the Jews then show themselves men, avoiding such abominations. Many Scripture prophecies, delivered long ago, are not yet fulfilled; but the fulfillment of some is an assurance that the rest will come to pass. Nothing can help more to make us easy than to be assured that God will do all his pleasure. Even those who do not know or care about God’s revealed will are called and used to fulfill the counsels of his secret will. Heaven and earth shall pass away sooner than one small part of the word of God. Obstinate sinners are addressed; they were far from acceptance, but they were summoned to listen to the word of the Lord. The salvation of a sinner begins with a humble and contrite heart that trembles at God’s word, with godly sorrow working true repentance, and faith in his mercy, through the obedience unto death of our Divine Surety. Christ, as the divine righteousness and salvation to his people, would come in the appointed time. His salvation abides in his church for all believers.
